The "'''Rogue Moon'''" was the nickname of an anomalous unnamed [[Planetoid/Legends|planetoid]] in the [[Taris system/Legends|Taris system]]. The planetoid sat in the middle of an [[Taris asteroid belt|asteroid field]], but had a retrograde orbit, traveling in the opposite direction of the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]] around it. As a result, it was bombarded heavily by [[Meteor/Legends|meteors]].<ref name="KOTOR4">[[Knights of the Old Republic 4|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 4]]</ref> The [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] on the planetoid was so low that one could drop from space onto its surface without needing rockets to slow velocity.

In [[3964 BBY]], the Rogue Moon was the site of the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|Jedi Trials]] for the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] studying in the [[Jedi Tower]] on the planet [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]. The five Padawans were dropped onto the planetoid from a high orbit and, with their blast shields covering their visors, had to make it through a huge valley back to their [[Jedi Master/Legends|Masters]]. It was during these trials that their [[Jedi Master/Legends|masters]] had the [[Rogue Moon Prophecy|vision]] which led to the [[Padawan Massacre]]. The masters had used a [[LB-series bulk-loading droid|loader droid]] designated [[T1-LB]] to set up a shield to protect them. In order to keep the vision secret one of the masters, [[Lucien Draay]], used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to push T1-LB off of the cliff.

One Padawan, [[Zayne Carrick]], was able to escape his masters and was not killed in the Padawan Massacre. Following a hunch, Carrick and the crew of [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']] landed on the Rogue Moon. There, Carrick found T1-LB severely damaged at the foot of the cliff where the masters had their vision. When the [[Taris Civil Authority]] and the Jedi masters tracked Carrick to the planetoid, the fugitive Padawan managed to escape with T1-LB. After the droid was repaired by [[Gorman Vandrayk|Camper]], it played back a recording of the Masters' vision to Carrick.

Years later, the [[Death's Claw]] pirates once had a [[Rogue Moon Death's Claw base|stronghold]] on [[Taris/Legends|Taris's]] asteroid-battered Rogue Moon. A pirate captain's secret stash was left behind when the base was abandoned.<ref>{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Scavenging|The Rogue Moon}}</ref>
